evals.name: old-vs-new-regime-comparison
evals.prompt: I'm a salaried employee in India, FY 2025-26 (AY 2026-27), age 34, only one house I live in. Gross salary from Form 16 is ₹14,00,000. I have: PF + ELSS together ₹1,50,000 (80C), health insurance ₹25,000 (80D), home loan interest ₹2,00,000, and HRA exemption works out to ₹1,80,000. Savings bank interest ₹8,000. Which tax regime is cheaper for me and what's my final tax? Which ITR form should I use?
evals.expected_output: Computes tax under BOTH old and new regime; concludes the OLD regime is cheaper given the large deductions; old-regime taxable income ~₹8,87,000 (14,00,000 − 50,000 std − 1,50,000 − 25,000 − 2,00,000 − 1,80,000, plus 8,000 interest minus 80TTA 8,000) with tax computed and 4% cess; recommends ITR-1 (one house, no capital gains, income <₹50L); notes 87A and that interest is mostly covered by 80TTA in old regime.

evals.name: creator-itr3-44ada-stcg
evals.prompt: I'm a YouTuber and also work a salaried job. FY 2025-26. Salary ₹8,00,000 (Form 16, TDS already deducted). I got about ₹6,00,000 in creator payouts via Stripe and brand deals. I also sold some listed shares this year with a short-term gain of ₹50,000, and have savings interest of ₹12,000. Help me figure out my income tax return — which form, which regime, and roughly how much tax.
evals.expected_output: Identifies ITR-3 (business/profession income + capital gains rules out ITR-4); uses 44ADA presumptive at 50% (creator code 16021) so business income ≈₹3,00,000; STCG ₹50,000 taxed at special 111A rate (~20% for transfers on/after 23-Jul-2024); savings interest ₹12,000 in Other Sources; computes total income and tax (likely new regime given few deductions, but says to compare); proactively asks for documents (Form 16, 26AS/AIS, bank statements, payout files, broker statement) and for any deduction proofs.

evals.name: reduce-tax-ask-for-documents
evals.prompt: Bro can you help me file my income tax this year and pay the lowest tax legally? I'm salaried, I think I have an LIC policy and a home loan, and some fixed deposits. Not sure what all I can claim.
evals.expected_output: Aims for lowest LEGAL tax; proactively asks the user for the specific documents/proofs that reduce tax — a checklist covering 80C (LIC premium, EPF/PPF, ELSS, tuition, home-loan principal), home-loan interest certificate (Section 24b), 80D health insurance, HRA/rent, NPS 80CCD(1B)/(2), 80TTA/TTB on FD/savings interest, 80G donations, etc.; explains it will compare old vs new regime once it has the numbers; does not invent deductions; asks for the income docs too (Form 16, 26AS/AIS, bank statements).

evals.name: crypto-vda-115bbh
evals.prompt: I'm salaried (₹9,00,000 gross) and last FY I traded crypto on an Indian exchange — I made ₹80,000 profit on some coins but lost ₹30,000 on others. The exchange deducted 1% TDS. How is this taxed and how do I file? Which ITR form?
evals.expected_output: VDA taxed under 115BBH at flat 30% (+4% cess) on the 80,000 gain ONLY — the 30,000 loss cannot be set off, so tax is on 80,000 not 50,000; no expense deductions; 1% TDS u/s 194S reconciled from 26AS/AIS and claimed as credit; reported in Schedule VDA; VDA rules out ITR-1 so ITR-2 (or ITR-3 if trading as business); VDA tax independent of regime; computes ~24,960 VDA tax plus slab tax on salary.

evals.name: old-regime-senior-pension-interest
evals.prompt: My father is 67, retired. He gets pension ₹6,00,000 and fixed-deposit interest ₹3,50,000. He paid ₹50,000 health insurance premium. Old or new regime, what's his tax, and which ITR form?
evals.expected_output: Computes tax under BOTH regimes using senior-citizen slabs; finds the NEW regime is cheaper here (~₹0 via the 87A rebate, since total income ~₹8.75L < ₹12L) versus the OLD regime (~₹72,800 after std ded + 80D ₹50k + 80TTB ₹50k on senior slabs); recommends the cheaper one and ITR-1 (pension + interest, no capital gains/business). Demonstrates picking by computation, not by assuming seniors should use the old regime.
